V-M-031.checks: Verify endpoint history distinguishes normal roaming from true ping-pong reuse, ignores NAT port-only changes, degrades safely without Redis, and auto-rotates configs only for confirmed abuse under cooldown control.
V-M-031.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_anti_ping_pong_detector.py tests/test_handshake_anomaly_detector.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_device_access_policy.py tests/test_device_audit_log.py tests/test_admin_device_control.py -v
V-M-031.last-run: 24 passed, 12 warnings. Command was run outside sandbox because sandboxed aiosqlite async SQLite initialization hangs.
V-M-031.anchors: VPN_ENDPOINT_ROAMING_OBSERVED VPN_ENDPOINT_PINGPONG_DETECTED VPN_ENDPOINT_MULTI_NETWORK_DETECTED VPN_ANTI_ABUSE_AUTO_ROTATE_STARTED VPN_ANTI_ABUSE_AUTO_ROTATE_COMPLETED VPN_ANTI_ABUSE_REDIS_DEGRADED VPN_ANTI_ABUSE_ENFORCEMENT_SKIPPED_COOLDOWN

scenarios.scenario-1: Single endpoint IP transition A to B is treated as roaming and does not rotate config.
scenarios.scenario-2: Alternating A to B to A to B within the configured window is classified as abuse.
scenarios.scenario-3: Four or more unique IPs inside the configured window produce a multi-network abuse signal.
scenarios.scenario-4: Auto-rotate preserves logical device identity, removes the old peer and makes a new config available.
scenarios.scenario-5: Redis outage must not trigger auto-rotation or block a device.
scenarios.scenario-6: Repeated abuse signals inside cooldown write evidence but do not rotate repeatedly.

module-checks.check: Classifier is deterministic and testable without a live AWG host.
Redis history keys use hashed peer identifiers and never store private keys, PSKs or raw configs.
Scheduler interval is configurable and idempotent across app restarts.
All durable events include user_id, device_id, event_type and safe context.

rollout-policy.step-1: Deploy with ANTI_ABUSE_MODE=observe and inspect events on live RU host.
rollout-policy.step-2: Enable ANTI_ABUSE_MODE=auto_rotate only after live evidence shows no false positives for normal roaming.
rollout-policy.step-3: Keep rollback simple: switch ANTI_ABUSE_MODE=observe or disabled and restart backend scheduler.
